def: v.t. To try, test, prove (syn. jāṅćnā).

s.m. Blackness; black colour; blackening; soot; smoke; black clothing; the black or inner part of the heart, the heart's core; the rural district (of any province or town), environs (of a city), suburbs; — a rough draft; reading; ability
corr. of sawād, q.v.
adj. Attentive, heedful, careful, cautious, circumspect; prudent; diligent
subs: (-se), To be careful or mindful (of), to beware (of)
v.t. To make (one) careful or cautious, to put (one) on (his) guard, to warn; — v.n. To take care, exercise caution, to be careful or cautious

def: s.f. = S ساودهانتو सावधानत्व sāvadhānatva,s.m. = H ساودهاني सावधानी sāvadhānī, s.f. Attention, carefulness, circumspection, caution, prudence.
s.m. = S ساودهانتا सावधानता sāvadhānatā, s.f. = H ساودهاني सावधानी sāvadhānī, s.f. Attention, carefulness, circumspection, caution, prudence.
s.f. = S ساودهانتا सावधानता sāvadhānatā, s.f. = S ساودهانتو सावधानत्व sāvadhānatva,s.m. Attention, carefulness, circumspection, caution, prudence.
(dialec.) = sabhoṅ, format. pl. of sab, q.v.
adj. Having the excess of a fourth, &c. ( = sawā, q.v.); — s.m. A title of the Rājā of Jaipūr.

s.f. Excess by a fourth; (or siwāʼī) a rate of interest on seed-grain advanced, a maund and a quarter to be repaid at harvest for every maund lent at sowing time; (or sawāʼī-kā rassā) the name of the rope (turned in four or in two directions) which is fastened to the top of a tent-pole; name of a measure in Prākrit verse (see sawāyā); — (local) the venereal disease.
adj. = sab+hī, emphat. of sab, q.v.
s.m. pl. (of sāʼima, v.n. fr. سوم 'to rove for pasture,' &c.), Cattle, or camels (and sheep or goats) pasturing by themselves or sent forth to pasture, and not fed with fodder at home.

adj. With a quarter more, &c. ( = sawāyā, q.v.); — s.m. A quarter more; a weight of 1 ¼ ser; the multiplication table of 1 ¼ times; a kind of verse or measure (consisting of four lines, each of 16+15 = 31 instants, the last foot being a trochee); a song in this measure; a singer of the sawaiyā
subs: s.m. A species of metre ( = sawaiyā).

def: adj. & s.m. Savage, wild; — a wild animal, sport, game (as deer, tiger, &c.).
s.m. Child; young of an animal; chick; fledgeling.
s.m. Young (of an animal); a child ( = sāwak, q.v.).
s.m. Leisure, opportunity; — adj. & adv. Having leisure, with leisure; — at leisure, leisurely.
s.m. Leisure, opportunity; — adj. & adv. Having leisure, with leisure; — at leisure, leisurely.
s.m. = sābal, q.v.
s.m. = sāhūl, q.v.
s.f. (dialec.) = śambālī, q.v.
s.m. corr. of 1˚ suʼāl, q.v.; — 2˚ siwālā, q.v.
s.m. The fourth Hindū month, July-August (beginning when the sun enters Cancer: its full moon is near Śravaṇā or α Aquilæ)
subs: s.m. Sunshine and rain; — a kind of lattice-work; — a kind of firework
s.f. The constant showers of Sāwan
'Flourishing in summer and not fading in autumn'; continuing in the same state or condition.
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi ساون (savn /⁠sāvan⁠/) (c. 1780), from Prakrit 𑀲𑀸𑀯𑀡 (sāvaṇa), from Sanskrit श्रावण (śrā́vaṇa). Cognate with Bengali শাওন (śaōn), Gujarati સાવણ (sāvaṇ), Punjabi ਸਉਣ (sauṇa) / ساون (sāvn), Sindhi سانوڻ (sānoṇ) / सांवणु (sā̃vaṇu). Compare Pashto ساوڼ (sāwaṇ).

def: s.m. Institutor of a sacrifice or employer of priests at a sacrifice; the conclusion of a sacrifice or the ceremonies by which it is terminated; — a name of Varuṇa; — a month of thirty solar days; a natural or civil day (from sunrise to sunset); a day reckoned from sunrise to sunrise (of which 1,577,917,828 are counted in a yuga or cycle of 4,320,000 years).
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi ساون (savn /⁠sāvan⁠/) (c. 1780), from Prakrit 𑀲𑀸𑀯𑀡 (sāvaṇa), from Sanskrit श्रावण (śrā́vaṇa). Cognate with Bengali শাওন (śaōn), Gujarati સાવણ (sāvaṇ), Punjabi ਸਉਣ (sauṇa) / ساون (sāvn), Sindhi سانوڻ (sānoṇ) / सांवणु (sā̃vaṇu). Compare Pashto ساوڼ (sāwaṇ).

def: s.m. The act of bearing children, bringing forth young, generation; the moon; — the act of extracting or pouring out the juice of the Soma plant; a libation; a sacrifice; drinking the soma juice; bathing, ablution (purificatory or expiatory).

s.f. The day of full moon in the month Sāwan; the harvest of Sāwan, the ḵẖarīf or autumn harvest; the rejoicings of the month of Sāwan (in which the scattered members of a family assemble under the paternal roof, and presents are interchanged between families where an engagement is pending); sweetmeats and fruits, &c. presented by the bridegroom (or by the parents) to the bride in Sāwan; the ceremony of presenting a fiancée with the materials for a swing (as coloured poles, ropes, &c.) in the month Sāwan; the materials for a swing which are presented to a fiancée in Sāwan.
s.m.f. (dialec.), Giver of a good omen.
adj. & s.f. pl. of s̤āniya, q.v.
s.f. corr. of sawārī, q.v.

s.m. A news-writer, an intelligencer (an officer stationed by the Mog̠al government in distant provinces to transmit weekly to the court an account of all public transactions, such as the collection of revenue, the management of lands, and the state of the country)
corr. of sawār, q.v.
v.t. = saṅwārnā, q.v.
adj. & s.m. Brave, warlike, heroic; — a leader, general; warrior, hero, champion.
s.f. Bravery, valour, heroism.
s.f. Lying-in chamber; impurity from childbirth (cf. the forms soṛ, sowaṛ, and sauṅr).
s.m. = شور śavar, q.v.
s.m. A barbarian, a savage; a member of a particular tribe of barbarians who inhabit the mountainous districts of India, and wear peacock's feathers as decoration; — name of Śivan-water.
1˚ = sābar or sābal; — 2˚ = sāwal or sāʼūl, qq.v.
s.m. = sawar or śavar, q.v.

ety: sawār, q.v.+ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ; cf. aswārī
def: s.f. Riding; horsemanship; anything on which one rides, vehicle (beast, horse, &c.), conveyance; equipage, cavalcade, suite; — a trick in wrestling
subs: s.f. A cess to defray the expense of entertaining some person of rank, or one in authority
(-ko), To give (one) a ride or a mount; to carry, &c.

def: s.f. (dialec.) = śambālī, q.v.
adj. (f. -ā), Of the same colour; of like appearance, like, resembling; of the same kind, or tribe, or class, homogeneous.
v.t. = saṅwārnā, q.v.
s.f. An epithet of Ćhāyā, wife of the sun.
s.m. Sameness or similiarity of colour; identity of caste or class.
adj. (Braj.) = sāṅwlā, q.v.
s.f. A young woman.
s.f. = saha-vasti, q.v.
s.f. = saha-vasti, q.v.
adj. (f. -ā), Together with a calf, having a calf; having offspring or descendants.
s.m. = sawaiyā, q.v.
s.m.f. Of the same age; coeval, contemporary; — a contemporary; a friend of the same age.
adj. Composed of parts, possessed of limbs or members.
adj. & adv. Early, timely; in good time, soon.
s.m. The early morning, dawn; — sawere (obl. form. of sawerā), adv. In the early morning, at dawn, early, soon.
adv. See s.v. sawerā (of which it is the format. sing.).
s.f. corr. of سوغات saug̠āt, q.v.
adj. Having the mystical letter or syllable of a magical formula at its commencement (a mantra, or form of prayer, &c.).
adj. Changed; transformed, transfigured.
adj. (f. -ā), Sportive, playful, wanton, amorous, coquettish.
adj. (f. -ā), Astonished, surprised; doubtful.
adj. contrac. of next, q.v.
s.m. The sun. (This is the name by which the deity is invoked in the Gāyatrī).
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀲𑁄𑀢𑁆𑀢 (sotta), from Sanskrit *स्रोत्र (*srotra), from स्रु (sru, “to flow”).

def: adj. Relating or belonging to the sun; descended from the sun, belonging to the solar dynasty; — s.m. The sun; — a name of Śiva; an epithet of Karṇa, the child of the sun; name of a Vasu; name of one of the Nakshatras or lunar asterisms.

s.f. A beam or ray of light; a cluster of solar rays; name of a celebrated verse of the Rig-veda (regarded as the most sacred verse of the Veda, and called Sāvitrī as addressed to the sun); the ceremony of investiture with the sacred thread; the sacrificial or sacred thread; — an epithet of Umā (wife of Śiva); and of the wife of Satyavān.
adj. Possessing discrimination, &c.
adj. & s.m. (dialec.) Having black ears; — a black-eared creature or animal.
v.t. (rustic) = sewnā, q.v.
adj. (f. -ā), Left, left-hand; to the left (cf. ćap); south, southern; reverse, contrary, backward.
s.m. = سايه sāya, q.v.
s.m. End, close, termination; close of day, evening; — an arrow
subs: vulg. sāyaṅ-kāl, q.v., s.m. Evening, eventide.

def: s.m. Shadow, shade; shelter, protection; apparition, spectre; influence (of an evil spirit); — a petticoat
subs: vulg. sāʼe-bān, s.m. Canopy; sunshade, umbrella, parasol; roof or fly of a tent; thatched roof
or sāya-parwarda, adj. & s.m. 'Brought up in the shade,' delicately nurtured; — a protégé; an inexperienced person; — a slave
(-par), The shadow (of one, -kā) to fall (upon); to come under the influence (of); to imitate
(-ke), or sāye-meṅ ānā (-ke), To come under the shadow or protection (of), to take shelter (with)
vulg. sāʼe-dār, adj. & s.m. Shady, umbrageous; affording shelter or protection; — a protector
(-par), To cast a shadow; to afford shelter or protection (to), to take under (one's) protection, to foster, to show favour (to); — sāya-figan, adj. & s.m. Casting a shadow; — shadow-caster
adj. & s.m. Shade-diffusing, shading, shady; sheltering, protecting; — shelterer, protector, favourer
(-ke), and sāya honā or ho jānā (-kā), To come under the shadow (of), to fall under the influence (of an evil spirit, or a fairy or jinn), to be possessed (by an evil spirit).

def: s.m. See s.v. سايه sāya.
corr. of shāyad, q.v.
s.m. An arrow; a dart; a sword; weapon.
s.m. An arrow; a dart; a sword.
s.m. = شيل śail, q.v.
adj. Sleepy; slothful, sluggish; — s.m. A snake; a boa-constrictor; a dog; a jackal.
s.m. Name of a learned Brāhmaṇ and commentator (he is thought to have flourished about A.D. 1370).
s.f. (dialec.) 1˚ = sain, 'sign'; 2˚ = senā; — s.m. (dialec.) = sewan, qq.v.
s.m. The longitude of a planet reckoned from the vernal equinoctial point.
s.m. Sleep, repose (see śayan)
s.m. Lying down, sleeping, reposing; sleep; a bed, couch; copulation
subs: (˚na+ić˚), adj. Desirous of sleep; desirous of copulation.

def: s.m. Sagacity, cleverness, shrewdness, cunning, artfulness; maturity.

adj. (f. -ī) & s.m. Grown up, adolescent, of age, mature (in understanding), arrived at puberty or years of discretion; knowinG, sagacious, prudent, clever, wise, intelligent; shrewd, cunning, artful, sly; — a knowing or astute person, a sharp or shrewd man; a wise man; a conjurer, an exorcist; a miser.
s.m. = H سيانپت सियानपत siyān-pat, स्यानपत syān-pat, s.f. = syānpan, q.v.
s.m. See s.v. sāya, 'end.'
s.f. corr. of ساعت sāʻat, q.v.
adj. & s.m. Possessing or enjoying the season of youth; — one who is experiencing the time or the condition of youth.
s.m. corr. of shāh-zāda, q.v.s.v. shāh.
s.m. Union, identification, intimate union, absorption (esp. the ultimate re-absorption of the soul into the divine essence); similarity, likeness.
s.f. = śayyā; and sej, qq.v.
s.f. A bed, couch, sofa (see sej).

ety: act. part of sāḵẖtan, rt. Zend śać = S. शक्
def: part. & s.m. Making, preparing, effecting; feigning; — maker; counterfeiter (used as last member of compounds; e.g. zinda-sāz, 'making alive,' 'resuscitating'; — ghaṛī-sāz, 'watch-maker'); — s.m. Arms, accoutrements; apparatus; instrument, implement; harness; furniture; ornament; concord, harmony; a musical instrument
subs: adj. Trim, dressy; — s.m. Apparatus, &c.; ornament; concord ( = sāz)
or sāz-gār, adj. Consonant, concordant, in accordance (with); proper, agreeing (with), harmonizing, suiting, befitting
or sāz-gārī, s.f. Concord, harmony, agreement; peace
v.t. To prepare, get ready (necessaries, &c. for); to put in order, to arrange; to cause to accord; to tune (an instrument)
s.m. Apparatus, instruments, implements; appliances; materials; furniture; necessaries, supplies

def: s.m. (local), corr. of sājhā, q.v.

ety: rt. Zend śać; cf. Pehl. śajak
def: s.f. (orig.) 'Worthy, deserving'; correction, chastisement, nunishment; penalty, retribution
subs: To receive punishment, to be punished
(-ko) or sazā karnā (-kī), To inflict punishment (on), to punish, chastise; to avenge
adj. & s.m. Who, or one who, has been punished; an old offender.

ety: imperf. part. of sāḵẖtan; and = sāz, q.v.+Zend añṭ = S. अन्त्
def: s.m. Maker; arranger, adjuster; — player on musical instruments, a musician.
s.m. Collector of revenue or rent, tax-gatherer, land-steward, bailiff; superintendent, supervisor, overseer.
adj. Relating to a sazāwal; — s.f. The business of a sazāwal.
adj. = sazāwār, q.v.
adj. Worthy, deserving, meritorious; becoming, fit, suitable, congruous, applicable, proper, excellent.
s.f. Worthiness, desert, merit, excellence; justness; fitness, suitableness, applicability.
s.f. Making, effecting, &c. (used as last member of comp., e.g. zinda-sāzī, 'resuscitation').
s.f. Combination, collusion, confederacy, league, cabal, conspiracy
adj. Collusive, fraudulent.

ablat. postpn. Of (denoting material, or race or stock, &c.); from; out of; with; in connection with; along with (e.g. bahut sāmān-se ānā); concerning; to (e.g. ghoṛā meḵẖ-se bandhā-hūʼā thā); by; by means of, by the instrumentality of; by way of, along; over; upon; at; by reason of, in consequence of, through; on account of; in reference to, in respect of, as regards; according to; since; for (a period past); between; among; than (marking the comparative degree).
ex: وہ روز اپنے پڑوسی سے لڑتا ہے۔
vo roz apne paṛosī se laṛtā hai.
He fights with his neighbor every day.
میں نے اپنی ماں سے پوچھا۔
ma͠i ne apnī mā̃ se pūchā.
I asked mother.
wety: Uncertain. Several etymologies have been proposed, and a merger of some (or even all) of them is extremely likely. * Inherited from Sanskrit समम् (samam, “together; similarly, as”), from सम (samá, “same”), from Proto-Indo-Aryan *samHás, from Proto-Indo-Iranian *samHás, from Proto-Indo-European *somHós (“same”). Compare Braj सौं (sa͠u, “to, with”). * Inherited from Sanskrit सङ्गे (saṅge), locative of सङ्ग (saṅgá, “…

def: s.f. = seh, or sehī, or sāhī, qq.v.
ex: وہ روز اپنے پڑوسی سے لڑتا ہے۔
vo roz apne paṛosī se laṛtā hai.
He fights with his neighbor every day.
میں نے اپنی ماں سے پوچھا۔
ma͠i ne apnī mā̃ se pūchā.
I asked mother.
wety: Uncertain. Several etymologies have been proposed, and a merger of some (or even all) of them is extremely likely. * Inherited from Sanskrit समम् (samam, “together; similarly, as”), from सम (samá, “same”), from Proto-Indo-Aryan *samHás, from Proto-Indo-Iranian *samHás, from Proto-Indo-European *somHós (“same”). Compare Braj सौं (sa͠u, “to, with”). * Inherited from Sanskrit सङ्गे (saṅge), locative of सङ्ग (saṅgá, “…

r: s̤e, Hindūstānī, th, thin, s, abjad
def: The sixth letter of the Hindūstānī alphabet (the fourth of the Arabic) occurs only in words borrowed from the Arabic. Its sound in Arabic is that of th in the English thin; but in Hindūstānī it has the sound of s. According to the abjad, q.v., it has the numerical value of 500.
format., or obl. case, sing. of sā, 'like,' q.v.
ex: وہ روز اپنے پڑوسی سے لڑتا ہے۔
vo roz apne paṛosī se laṛtā hai.
He fights with his neighbor every day.
میں نے اپنی ماں سے پوچھا۔
ma͠i ne apnī mā̃ se pūchā.
I asked mother.
wety: Uncertain. Several etymologies have been proposed, and a merger of some (or even all) of them is extremely likely. * Inherited from Sanskrit समम् (samam, “together; similarly, as”), from सम (samá, “same”), from Proto-Indo-Aryan *samHás, from Proto-Indo-Iranian *samHás, from Proto-Indo-European *somHós (“same”). Compare Braj सौं (sa͠u, “to, with”). * Inherited from Sanskrit सङ्गे (saṅge), locative of सङ्ग (saṅgá, “…

def: (Braj) = sā, 'like,' q.v.
ex: وہ روز اپنے پڑوسی سے لڑتا ہے۔
vo roz apne paṛosī se laṛtā hai.
He fights with his neighbor every day.
میں نے اپنی ماں سے پوچھا۔
ma͠i ne apnī mā̃ se pūchā.
I asked mother.
wety: Uncertain. Several etymologies have been proposed, and a merger of some (or even all) of them is extremely likely. * Inherited from Sanskrit समम् (samam, “together; similarly, as”), from सम (samá, “same”), from Proto-Indo-Aryan *samHás, from Proto-Indo-Iranian *samHás, from Proto-Indo-European *somHós (“same”). Compare Braj सौं (sa͠u, “to, with”). * Inherited from Sanskrit सङ्गे (saṅge), locative of सङ्ग (saṅgá, “…

def: s.m. An apple; (poet.) the chin
s.m. Sprinkling, aspersion, watering, irrigation; dripping; baling; — a baling-vessel, a bucket.
s.f. A baling-vessel, a bucket.
corr. of صحت ṣěḥḥat, q.v.

ety: sed˚ = S. स्वेदय(ति), caus. of rt. स्विद्
def: v.t. To cause to sweat, to make warm, to foment, to stupe.
s.f. = H سيگوٿا सेगोटा seg̠oṭā, s.m. Soil consisting of a mixture of sand and clay.
s.m. = H سيگون सेगौन segaun, s.f. Soil consisting of a mixture of sand and clay.
s.m. (dialec.) Teak-wood ( = sāgaun, q.v.).
v.t. To whisk.

ety: for A. صحة, inf. n. of صحّ 'to be healthy,' &c.
def: s.f. Health, soundness of body; restoration to health; sound or valid or true state or condition; soundness, perfection, entireness, validity, integrity, accuracy, correctness, authenticity; confirmation
subs: v.n. To be restored to health, to recover (from sickness), to get well
s.m. A certificate of health; — a list or table of corrections, corrigenda
ex: شَراب نوشی صِحَت کے لِیے مُضِر ہَے۔
śarāb nośī sihat ke liye muzir hai.
Wine-drinking is harmful for health.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian صِحَّت (sihhat), from Arabic صِحَّة (ṣiḥḥa).

ety: sīh, q.v.+ī = Prk. इआ = S. इका
def: s.f. A porcupine; hedgehog.
s.f. A necklace of thread, &c. ( = selī, q.v.).
v.t. (dialec.), 1˚ = sahnā, q.v.; — 2˚ = sewnā, q.v.

ety: prob. Prk. सक्कलओ; S. शकल, or शल्कल+कः
def: s.m. Scale of a fish (syn. sarhnā).
s.m. A chaplet, &c. ( = sihrā or sěhrā, q.v.).
s.m. The milk hedge-plant, Euphorbia antiquorum; also E. neriifolia; and E. tirucalli; and E. nivulia.
s.f. A bed, couch; bedding; a layer of straw (for a horse, &c.)
subs: 'To spread out bedding,' to make a bed
s.m. A cord for fastening bedding, or a sheet, to a bedstead
s.m. (f. -ā), One who is a connoisseur in the matter of a good or comfortable bed
'To mount a bed,' to go to bed
or sej saṅwārnā, 'To prepare, or arrange, a bed,' to make a bed.

def: s.f. = sej, q.v.
s.f. = seṅk, q.v.
s.f. A skate (for sliding).
adj. & s.m. = ses, and śesh, qq.v.
s.f. = śekhar, q.v.
s.m. A crest, chaplet, diadem, crown; a garland of flowers worn on the top of the head; — peak, summit (of a mountain).
s.f. A dish of curds and sugar with spices.
v.t. = seṅknā, q.v.
s.m. A spear, dart, javelin; — (dialec.) a species of carp.
s.m. A shell.

ety: prob. akin to S. चैलं, चेलं
def: s.m. A kind of scarf or mantle (of silk, or lawn, or muslin; usually composed of four breadths depending from the shoulders loosely over the body: it is much worn, and given as a present, in the Dakkhan); a silk turban.
s.m. A lion; a royal tiger.
s.m. A kind of rice; — rice boiled in the husk.
s.m. A spear, javelin ( = sel).

s.f. A necklace (of threads, silk, or hair, &c.) worn by faqīrs; a neck-ornament; — a line of hair extending from the breast to the navel, or across the middle of the belly (syn. romāwalī); — a rope; a sash, a belt; — (local) a discoloration in the face; a mole; a tetter.
s.f. A cat, tabby, puss.
s.f. 'Mountain-earth'; a species of steatites; soap-stone.
s.f. The flat or broad bean, Dolichos lablab
subs: The Goa bean, or the chevaux-de-frise bean, Psophocarpus tetragonolobus

def: s.m.(f.?) Leprosy producing discoloration of the skin.
s.m. The silk-cotton tree, Bombax heptaphyllum, and the cotton it yields
subs: The cotton of the semal-tree, silk-cotton

def: s.f. pl. = siwaiyāṅ, q.v.
s.f. An army.
s.m. A hawk, a falcon.
postpn. = se, q.v.
s.f. contrac. of sendh, q.v.
s.m. The commander of a body of troops; — an officer who collects the revenue (in a village).
v.t. = سيونا sewnā, q.v.
s.f. An army, armament, force, body of troops; an army personified as the wife of Kārttikeya, god of war
subs: senā-pat, and senā-p, s.m. 'Army-chief'; a general, military chief; a title of the Rājā of Berār; a title of Prime Ministers
sen-jitā (for senā-jitā), s.f. Name of a courtesan (apsarā) of Indra's paradise
s.m. Military chief, commander of a force, officer
senā-sthal, s.m. A place where an army is collected
s.m. Leader of an army, generalissimo, commander: a name of Kārttikeya, god of war; — adj. Appertaining to an army, military
s.m. (f. -ī), A person belonging to an army.

def: s.m. See s.v. senā, 'army.'
s.m. = semal, q.v.

ety: Prk. सिम्भओ and सेम्भओ; S. श्र्लेष्मकः, rt. श्र्लिष्
def: s.m. Rheum, defluxion; the watery or phlegmatic humour.
s.f. = seṅdh, q.v.
s.f. The Cucumis madraspatanus ( = seṭu); C. momordica (syn. phūṭ): — seṅd-kaćriyā s.m. Idem.
s.f. (dialec.) = sehunḍ, q.v.
s.f. A hole made in a wall by thieves or burglars; house-breaking ( = seṅdh-ćorī)
subs: s.f. House-breaking (by making a hole in the wall), burglary
seṅdh lagānā, or seṅdh mārnā (-meṅ), To make a hole (in the wall of a house), to break (into), to commit a burglary; to sap, mine

def: s.m. White rock-salt (found in the country near the Indus)
s.m. Burglar, &c. ( = seṅdhiyā).
s.f. The juice of the wild date tree or Phœnix sylvestris from which toddy is made; palm-wine, toddy.
s.f. = seṅd, q.v.
s.m. A miner, house-breaker, burglar ( = seṅdh-mār).
s.m. Poison ( = sankhiyā).
s.m. Name of a Marhata tribe (prob. so named as originating on the banks of the river Sindh).
s.m. A miner, house-breaker, burglar ( = seṅdh-mār).
v.t. To sap, mine, bore, dig, excavate.
s.f. = seṅd, and seṅdhī, q.v.

ety: seṅd˚ = Prk. सिंद(इ) or सिंदे(इ) = S. स्यन्दय(ति), caus. of rt. स्यन्द्
def: v.t. (dialec.) To bore for water; to draw (water); to irrigate.
s.m. = seṅdūr, q.v.
s.m. Minium, red lead, vermilion.
s.f. A red species of mango.

ety: S. सङ्गरं; or श्टङ्ग+ रं and र+इका
def: The capsule of a legume, a pod, siliqua (seṅgrī is said to be particularly applied to the siliqua of the radish).
s.m. A tetter, ringworm, a particular kind of cutaneous disease.
s.m. = sehunḍ; and sīj, qq.v.
postpn. (dialec.) = sang; and se, qq.v.
s.f. Warming, fomentation; warmth; toasting; basking; — the hooked iron instrument with which bread is drawn from ovens
subs: v.t. To warm, foment; to toast ( = seṅknā).

def: v.t. To warm, heat, foment; to air by warming; to dry before a fire; to toast, parch, bake; to incubate.
s.f. = selī, q.v.
s.f. = sewatī, q.v.
s.f. = سيٿ seṭ, q.v.
s.m. = seṭh, q.v.
s.f. = سيٿ seṭ, q.v.

r: seṅṭhā, seṅṭhī, moṛhās, Saccharum sara, sarkanḍā
def: s.m. = H سينٿهي सेंठी seṅṭhī, s.f. A kind of reed-grass (of which chairs or moṛhās are made), Saccharum sara (syn. sarkanḍā).

r: seṅṭhī, seṅṭhā, moṛhās, Saccharum sara, sarkanḍā
def: s.f. = H سينٿها सेंठा seṅṭhā, s.m. A kind of reed-grass (of which chairs or moṛhās are made), Saccharum sara (syn. sarkanḍā).
s.f. (dialec.) = siwaiyāṅ, q.v.
s.f. = sewtī or seʼotī, q.v.
s.m. The plant Trophis aspera.
s.m. The male organ, penis; a testicle.
s.m. The male organ, penis; a testicle.
s.m. The male organ, penis; a testicle.
s.f. corr. of śephālikā, q.v.
s.m. = śep, q.v.

ety: prob. akin to Zend thrãf; S. तर्फ् (तृफति)
def: adj. Full, satiated, sated; satisfied, contented; disgusted, sick or tired (of)
subs: adj. Full of water, saturated; moist, succulent; satisfied with drink; satisfied; — fresh, blooming; fertile, rich
v.t. To water, to saturate; to satisfy; to cause to flourish, &c.
s.m. A kind of food made of curds and garlic
s.f. Moistness, humidity, moisture, juiciness, succulence; freshness, bloom; fertility
adj. & s.m. Satisfied, contented; generous, liberal; — a contented person; a generous or liberal man (opp. to tang-ćashm)
adj. Very productive or fertile, very rich
v.n. To be sated, be cloyed, to be sick (of); to be distressed.

def: s.m. A weight of about two pounds, a 'seer' (the Calcutta factory ser, 1 lb. 13 oz. 13·86 drs. Avoirdupois; and the bāzār ser, 2 lbs. 0 oz. 13·853 drs.)
s.m. corr. of sher, q.v.
adj. = P سيرابي ser-ābī, s.f. See s.v. ser, 'full.'
s.f. = P سيراب ser-āb, adj. See s.v. ser, 'full.'
adj. (dialec.), Cold, &c. (see śītal).
v.t. To cause to float, to set afloat, to launch; to cause to go, to despatch; — to extend, to increase, augment.
v.t. To make cold, to cool ( = sirānā, q.v.).
s.m. corr. of sar-hang, q.v.
s.m. Sail (of a ship).
s.f. Satisfying of hunger, satiety, repletion, fulness; glut, plenitude; satisfaction, contentment
s.f. The ser-weight.
s.m. The head and foot parts of a bed-frame.

adj. Remaining, other, all the other; the last; — s.m. Remainder, rest, residue, leavings; end; appendix (see śesh); — name of a celebrated mythological thousand-headed serpent regarded as the emblem of eternity (he is called the king of the Nāgas or snakes inhabiting Pātāla, and is represented as forming the couch and canopy of Vishṇu whilst sleeping during the intervals of creation, and sometimes as bearing the entire world on one of his heads)
subs: ses-avasthā, s.f. The last state or condition of life, old age
s.m. 'The sleeper on the serpent Ses,' an epithet of Vishṇu

def: s.m. The gripe of a bow; — name of a game at cards.
adj. & s.m. = ses, and śesh, qq.v.
s.f. The remains of flowers or other offerings made to an idol and afterwards distributed amongst the worshippers and attendants.

adj. & s.m. Remaining; other, all the other; last, final; — remainder, residue, leavings; rest; arrears; surplus; balance; — result, issue, effect; conclusion, end, termination; finish; destruction; death; killing; — name of a celebrated mythological thousand-headed serpent (see ses or sesh, and ses-nāg); name of Bala-rām or Baldev
subs: (˚sha+av˚), s.f. The last state or condition of life, old age
s.f. Assimilation of residue; reduction of fractions of residues or successive fractional remainders
s.f. The last watch of the night
s.m. 'Sleeping on the serpent Śesh,' an epithet of Vishṇu
s.m. The last time; the last term; the time of end or death, the latter end

s.m. The serpent Śesh, the king of the serpent race, and of Pātāl (as the abode of snakes. He is represented as having a thousand heads; and as forming the couch and canopy of Vishṇu whilst sleeping during the intervals of creation; and sometimes as bearing the entire world on one of his heads. He is also called An-anta 'the endless or infinite'; and as such is regarded as an emblem of eternity).

def: s.m. See s.v. śesh.
s.m. See s.v. ses, and śesh.
s.m. A mound, &c. ( = setu, q.v.)
adj. White
subs: s.m. 'White island'; one of the minor divisions of the world (identified geographically by Wilford with Britain)
s.m. A white crow; a rook.

def: s.f. (dialec.), A leech (syn. joṅk).
s.f. The hair about the groin or pubes; or that of the armpits; or that of the nostrils.
s.m. A great merchant; a banker, a money-broker; a millionaire; a capitalist; the chief of a corporation or trade; a title of respect given to merchants, bankers, &c. ( = seṭh-jī).
s.f. The wife of a Seṭh, q.v.
s.f. A whisk, a kind of brush.
= H سيتين सेतीं setīṅ, postpn. (dialec.) = se, q.v. (cf. sitī).
= H سيتي सेती setī, postpn. (dialec.) = se, q.v. (cf. sitī).

s.m. A ridge of earth, a mound, bank, causeway, dike, dam; a raised piece of ground separating fields serving as a boundary, or as a passage during inundations); a landmark; a bridge; a mountain road or track, a pass, defile
subs: or setu-bandh-rāmeśvar, s.m. 'Rām's bridge,' the ridge of rocks extending from the south extremity of the Coromandel coast towards the island of Ceylon (supposed to have been formed by Hanumān as a bridge for the passage of Rām's forces against Rāvan).

def: s.m. A water-melon; a kind of cucumber, Cucumis madraspatanus.
s.f. A kind of sweetmeat; dessert.
s.m. An apple ( = seb).
s.f. contrac. of sevā, q.v.

s.f. Service, servitude, tending, attendance (on, -kī); employment, situation, office, post, province; honouring, adoration, worship, homage; piety; devotion, addiction (to, -kī); use, practice; resorting (to), frequentation
subs: (-kī), To serve; to attend (on), wait (on); to worship, &c.

def: s.f. The male organ, penis ( = śeva, śep).
s.m. = P. شيوه shewa, q.v.
s.m. A snake; happiness; the penis ( = śep).
prep. corr. of siwā, q.v.
s.m. One of the nine treasures of Kuver, a divine treasure; a treasure.
prep. corr. of siwaʼe, q.v.
s.m. corr. of sevak, q.v.
s.f. (dialec.) = siwaiyāṅ, q.v.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā), Serving; servile; dependent; — a servant, attendant; a worshipper, a votary; a disciple; follower; dependent; a loyal person, subject
subs: s.m. A deed or bond by which a person binds himself to servitude (either for a term or for life).

def: s.f. Service, &c. ( = sevā, q.v.); discipleship; pupilage.

ety: sevak+S. इनी; and ई, or इका
def: s.f. = sevikā, q.v.

ety: sevak+S. इनी; and ई, or इका
def: s.f. = sevikā, q.v.
s.f. The ceremony of waving over the heads of a bride and bridegroom an imitation lamp made of flour, and also some water, which is then thrown on either side of them.
s.m. The aquatic plant Vallisneria octandra (see siwār).
s.m. The act of following; serving, service ( = sevā); devotion (to), addiction (to); using, use (syn. istěʻmāl), practising, practice
subs: (-kā), To serve; to be devoted or addicted (to); to make use (of), to practise.

def: part. adj. To be served, requiring or deserving service or attendance; worshipful; — s.m. A master, &c.
s.m. = siwār, q.v.
s.f. Sugar that has been purified with sewār or siwār, q.v.
adj. Tended, well-cared for, in good condition, plump, fat.
s.m. (f.? — dialec.), End, extremity; summit; termination, conclusion, close.
adj. (dialec.), Extreme, last; conclusive, closing, final, definitive.
s.f. A white rose; the dog-rose; China rose; Rosa glandulifera (syn. gul-ě-nasrīn).
adj. & s.m. = sevak, q.v.
s.f. A female servant; disciple; votary, worshipper.
s.f. (dialec.) = siwaiyāṅ, q.v.
part. Served, attended, waited on, honoured, worshipped; frequented.

ety: fr. S. सेव˚, rt. सिव् 'to unite'
def: s.m. Name of a caste whose business it is to extract the juice of the date or palm (tāṛ) trees (so named from their climbing with a sīwan or loop passed round their feet).

ety: sew˚ = Prk. सेव(इ) = S. सेव(ते), rt. सेव्
def: v.t. To attend on, serve; to adore, worship; to heed, give attention to; to protect, watch over, guard; to brood, incubate, rear, hatch, sit on (eggs)
subs: To sit on eggs, to brood; (fig.) to mope.

def: s.m. = سيوڙا sewṛā, q.v.
s.m. A faqīr of the Jain sect; — a conjurer.
s.m. Half-baked earthenware.
s.m. Any faqīr of the Jain sect.
s.f. Name of a female worshipper of Rāmćandra.
adj. = شيويه śaivya, q.v.
part. adj. To be served, deserving or requiring to be served, or worshipped, or obeyed, &c.; honourable, worshipful; estimable, respectable.

ety: Pārsī saw; old P. khsapa; Pehl. shap; Zend khshap; S. क्षप्
def: s.f. Night
subs: adv. In the night-time, during the night; all night
s.m. A person who exhibits various figures for amusement at night, a puppet-showman; one who is up or vigilant at night; a bat
s.m. One who stays all night, a night lodger
s.f. Staying all night; a night's halt or lodging
May the night pass well, good night; salutation at night

s.f. The eve of the 14th day of the month Shaʻbān (on which a vigil is observed with prayers, feastings, illuminations, &c., and the Musalmāns make offerings and oblations in the names (if not to the manes) of deceased ancestors; on this night the lives and fortunes of mortals during the coming year are said to be registered in heaven)
(and gul-ě-shab-bo), s.m. (f.?) lit. 'Night-smelling,' the tuberose, Polianthes tuberosa
adj. Waking or watching all night; sleepless
or shab-parak, or shab-para, s.m. lit. 'Night-flier'; a bird of night (as a bat, an owl, &c.); see shappara
s.m. lit. 'Night-shining'; a fire-fly; a glow-worm; a gem, &c. that sparkles in the dark
s.m. lit. 'Night-lamp'; a carbuncle; a gem of the ruby kind; a sparkling or brilliant gem
s.m. 'The little night-lamp'; a fire-fly; a glow-worm
s.m. A night-attack; — shab-ḵẖūn mārnā (-par), To make a night-attack (on)
s.f. Robbery at night with bloodshed
adj. & s.m. Rising at night (to pray); pious, devout; — one who rises at night to pray
s.f. A dish of meat and vegetables (esp. turnips) dressed all night on the fire and eaten the next morning
adj. & s.m. Overtaken by night, benighted; — one who is benighted
adj. Dusky or dark-coloured, black (a horse); — s.m. Name of a horse of Siyāvush
s.m. One who walks or travels in the night; a night-watch; a thief
s.f. The night of the tenth of Moharram (on which Husain was slain)
s.f. 'The night of power,' the 27th of Ramazān (when the Qorʼān is said to have descended from heaven; — syn. lailatuʼl-qadr)
s.f. Night-blindness, nyctalopia (syn. rat-aundhā); — shab-gāh, s.f. Night-place to keep anything in; night-time
s.f. Going rounds by night, patrolling, watching at night
s.f. Nocturnal perambulation, patrolling; a procession at night
adj. Of the colour of night, dark as night, black

s.m. One who passes a sleepless night; a cricket; a nightingale; any animal that chirps or sings at night; — travelling after midnight and before sunrise; a servant that works or travels in the night-time; — early dawn; — attacking by night, night-march, nocturnal invasion
s.f. 'Night-moisture,' dew (syn. os); — s.m. A kind of fine linen, a very fine muslin
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شب (šab). Cognate of Sanskrit क्षप् (kṣap).

ety: inf. n. of شبع 'to be satiated,' &c.
def: s.f. Fulness, satiety.
s.m. Jet; black or dark coral; agate; a bead.

ety: inf. n. of شبّ 'to become a youth'
def: s.m. Youth, youthfulness, prime of manhood (supposed to be the state from sixteen to thirty-two).
s.m.f. A similar thing, a like, fellow, match, an analogue; an image, similitude, likeness, resemblance

ety: for A. شباهة, v.n. fr. شبه; see shabīh
def: s.f. Similarity, similitude, likeness, resemblance.
s.m. pl. (of shabaka), Nets, &c.

ety: for A. شبکة, v.n. fr. شبك 'to make reticulated,' &c.
def: s.m. A net; lattice; trellis; grating; a reticulated veil, &c. (syn. jāl, jālī).

ety: v.n. fr. تشعّب, 'to be separated,' v of شعب 'to separate'
def: s.m. The eighth month of the Arabian year (the 14th day of this month is the festival called barāt or shab-barāt; on this day an angel is supposed to examine a record on which is written the names of the living).
adj. & adv. Nocturnal; — by night
subs: s.m. & adv. Night and day, the civil day of twenty-four hours ( = Gr. νυχθημερον).

def: s.f. The evening; a lodging-place for the night; a fold (for cattle or sheep).
s.f. The evening; a lodging-place for the night; a fold (for cattle or sheep).
s.m. & adv. = shabāna-roz, q.v.
intj. corr. of shābāsh, q.v.

ety: shād, q.v.+bāsh, imperat. of būdan, 'to be'
def: intj. Well done! bravo! excellent!
s.f. Praise, applause, plaudits

ety: act. part. of شبّ 'to become a youth,' &c.
def: adj. & s.m. Youthful, in the prime of manhood; — a young man, a youth.
s.m. Alum.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شب (šab). Cognate of Sanskrit क्षप् (kṣap).

def: s.m. (f.?) = P شبگير shab-gīr, s.m. = S شبنم shab-nam, s.f. See s.v. shab.
s.m. lit. 'Night-colour'; a horse of a dark rusty colour, a black horse; a horse of noble breed; name of a famous black horse belonging to King Khusrau Parwez.
s.m. (f.?) See s.v. shab.
s.f. (local), corr. of shabīh, q.v.

ety: v.n. of شبه; ii, شبّه 'to make like, to liken'
def: adj. Like, resembling; — s.f. The like (of); resemblance, &c. ( = shibh, q.v.); image, picture, portrait

ety: shab, q.v.+īna = Zend aff. aêna = S. इन्
def: adj. Nocturnal, nightly; last night's; stale.
s.m. See s.v. shab.
s.f. (local) dim. of shabaka, q.v.; — shabkiyāṅ, s.f. pl. Lattices; venetian blinds.
s.m. (f.?) See s.v. shab.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شَبْنَم (šabnam). By surface analysis, شَب (śab, “night”) + نَم (nam, “moisture”).

ety: Zend shāta, perf. part. of shā, 'to rejoice,' &c.
def: adj. Pleased, delighted, exulting, joyful, happy, glad, cheerful, rejoicing (in); (in comp.) full (of); much
subs: adj. Full of water or moisture, moist, humid, succulent; fresh, verdant; pleasant, agreeable
adj. Fortunate, well off; voluptuous; — s.f. A dancing-girl, an adulteress, a strumpet
adj. Rejoicing, exulting; joyous, happy, contented

def: adj. = shaṭ, q.v.
adj. Joyful, glad, &c. ( = shād, q.v.); — s.m. Wine.
adj. See s.v. shād.
s.f. pl. (of شديدة, fem. of shadīd, q.v.), Difficult matters or things, difficulties; hardships, adversities, troubles, tribulations; disagreeable events.

ety: shād, q.v.+ān = Zend aff. āna or ana = S. अन
def: adj. Pleased, cheerful, glad (of, -se), &c. ( = shād, q.v.).
